title: Boolean expressions should not be gratuitous
description: Control flow constructs like if-statements allow the programmer to direct the flow of a program depending on a boolean expression. However, if the condition is always true or always false, only one of the branches will ever be executed. In that case, the control flow construct and the condition no longer serve a purpose; they become gratuitous.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Remove gratuitous boolean expressions - condition is always true or false
